Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Beyond likes and reach: Brands shift focus to real impact of creator campaigns
Article Text: Brands are now trying to measure the real impact of their creator campaigns, moving beyond follower counts and reach. Filling the gap, Cre8r.ai has introduced a new reporting tool to help track the actual results of creator-led activity.The firm’s CEO & founder, Gaurav Sharma said, "Influencer marketing has long been measured through surface metrics that don't translate into business outcomes. We are focusing on signals like sentiment, engagement quality, and intent — to help brands move beyond visibility."Data shared with the launch shows that higher-performing campaigns often have sentiment scores of 4.0 or above on a five-point scale. Purchase intent in top influencer campaigns is estimated at 3% to 5%. It also notes that 72% of social media users research products on platforms before making a purchase.
Article Summary: Cre8r.ai has introduced a new reporting tool to help track the actual results of creator-led activity. The firm's CEO & founder, Gaurav Sharma said, "Influencer marketing has long been measured through surface metrics that don't translate into business outcomes"

Article Title: Dyson launches Supersonic Travel hair dryer with compact design
Article Text: Dyson Supersonic Travel hair dryer is now official. Dyson has expanded its product lineup with the launch of Supersonic Travel hair dryer which is a compact styling tool engineered specifically for modern travellers. Designed with global voltage compatibility, the new dryer delivers Dyson’s signature fast styling and hair protection in a portable form factor, ensuring smoother and shinier hair wherever you go. 32% smaller and 25% percent lighter than Dyson’s full-sized Supersonic hair dryer, the new hair dryer is carry-on compatible, fitting easily into handbags, carry-ons and cabin luggage.Founder and chief engineer James Dyson emphasised that the Supersonic Travel is not just a smaller version of the original: “We didn’t just shrink a dryer; we re-engineered it for travel. The Supersonic Travel delivers high-performance styling while protecting hair from extreme heat.”Despite its compact size, the Supersonic Travel delivers the same effortless drying performance Dyson is known for. It features intelligent heat control technology that measures airflow temperature 100 times per second, preventing extreme heat damage. The airflow is precisely engineered to dry hair quickly and evenly, keeping it healthy and glossy.Another standout feature is attachment compatibility. Travellers can use their existing Dyson Supersonic or Supersonic Nural attachments with the new model, ensuring styling flexibility on the go.Since the launch of the original Supersonic in 2016, Dyson has redefined hair dryer design by placing the high-speed motor in the handle for better balance and introducing intelligent heat control to protect natural shine. The Supersonic Nural, launched earlier, added scalp protection features. The new travel edition extends this legacy, bringing Dyson’s core technologies into a smaller, lighter form factor without compromising results.The Dyson Supersonic Travel hair dryer is being introduced in select markets, with launch details for India to be announced closer to availability.
Article Summary: The Dyson Supersonic Travel hair dryer is 32% smaller and 25% lighter than the full-sized model. It is carry-on compatible, fitting easily into handbags, carry-ons and cabin luggage. It features intelligent heat control technology that measures airflow temperature 100 times per second, preventing extreme heat damage.

Article Title: Adobe unveils CX Enterprise for the agentic AI era at Adobe Summit 2026
Article Text:
Adobe AI summit

Price and availability



Adobe CX Enterprise features







At the Adobe Summit conference, Adobe announced the launch of CX Enterprise, a new end-to-end agentic AI system designed to redefine customer experience orchestration (CXO). The platform integrates AI agents, agent skills, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoints with an intelligence and governance layer, enabling businesses to deliver reliable, auditable, and highly personalized brand experiences at scale.Adobe positioned CX Enterprise as the next step in modern marketing, moving beyond isolated AI use cases into fully agentic enterprises. The system is powered by Adobe Brand Intelligence, a continuously learning reasoning engine that captures evolving brand signals, and Adobe Engagement Intelligence, a decisioning engine optimized for customer lifetime value. Together, they allow businesses to orchestrate personalized customer journeys while ensuring compliance with brand guidelines.While Adobe has not disclosed specific pricing, CX Enterprise will be available globally as part of Adobe’s enterprise suite. It will integrate seamlessly with Adobe Experience Platform (AEP), which already powers over one trillion customer experiences annually, serving as the contextual layer for CX Enterprise agents.* New AI Agents: Streamline tasks across customer engagement, content supply chains, and brand visibility.Integrated natively into Adobe solutions and extended to partner platforms like Amazon Quick, Anthropic Claude Enterprise, ChatGPT Enterprise, Gemini Enterprise, IBM watsonx Orchestrate, and Microsoft 365 Copilot.* Agent Skills Catalog: Reusable instructions packaged for workflows such as performance reviews, content creation, and journey orchestration. Businesses can customize skills with additional context to reflect organizational needs.* Developer Tools: Access to agentic skills, MCP servers, and infrastructure to build custom use cases. Designed for interoperability with Anthropic, Google Cloud, Microsoft, OpenAI, and more.* CX Enterprise Coworker: A coordination layer that translates business goals into multi-step actions. For example, it can assemble audience segments, creative assets, and performance insights to execute a targeted campaign aimed at boosting cross-sell performance.Adobe emphasized its commitment to an open ecosystem, announcing expanded partnerships with AWS, Anthropic, Google Cloud, IBM, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and OpenAI.
Article Summary: Adobe announced the launch of CX Enterprise, a new end-to-end agentic AI system. The platform integrates AI agents, agent skills, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoints with an intelligence and governance layer. The system is powered by Adobe Brand Intelligence and Adobe Engagement Intelligence.

Article Title: Earthset Video Shot On iPhone: NASA astronaut shares ‘Earthset’ video shot on Apple iPhone during Artemis II mission
Article Text:
‘Earthset’ video shot on Apple iPhone



Artemis II mission images taken on iPhone 17 Pro Max





NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman released a video of “Earthset” captured during the Artemis II mission which returned on April 11. The 53-second long video shows Earth disappearing behind the pockmarked moon. Sharing the video in an X post, Wiseman said it was captured using an Apple iPhone at 8x zoom. Explaining the video, he wrote: “Only one chance in this lifetime…Like watching sunset at the beach from the most foreign seat in the cosmos, I couldn’t resist a cell phone video of Earthset.” “I could barely see the Moon through the docking hatch window but the iPhone was the perfect size to catch the view…this is uncropped, uncut with 8x zoom which is quite comparable to the view of the human eye. Enjoy,” he added.As mentioned above, the video was shot on an iPhone 17 Pro Max that the astronauts carried on the mission. NASA approved the use of personal smartphones aboard Artemis II, making it one of the first missions where astronauts have brought their own handsets into space. The astronauts onboard carried four units of iPhone 17 Pro Max.The video highlights how small the Earth appears when seen from space, offering a clear sense of scale.It takes about eight seconds for a small section of the blue-and-white planet to come into view. The visuals show a sharp contrast between the dark space, the bright colours of Earth and the grey surface of the moon.As the video continues, the Earth gradually appears as a bright white sphere, with its blue colour fading. Over the next 20 seconds, the planet slowly moves out of view, completing what is described as an “Earthset.”In the video, Wiseman explains how Koch is using the 400mm lens of a Nikon camera to capture the moment. “You can hear the shutter on the Nikon as @Astro_Christina is hammering away on 3-shot brackets and capturing those exceptional Earthset photos through the 400mm lens. @AstroVicGlover was in window 3 watching with @Astro_Jeremy next to him,” he explained.Earlier this month, NASA released a series of striking images captured on the iPhone 17 Pro Max during the Artemis II mission, marking the first time consumer smartphones have been officially used to document a crewed lunar mission. The photos, taken on April 2, 2026, show astronauts Reid Wiseman and Christina Koch aboard the Orion spacecraft, gazing back at Earth through its cabin windows.“NASA astronaut and Artemis II mission specialist Christina Koch peers out of one of the Orion spacecraft's main cabin windows, looking back at Earth, as the crew travels towards the Moon,” wrote NASA while sharing the image on Flickr.
Article Summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman released a video of “Earthset” captured during the Artemis II mission. The 53-second long video shows Earth disappearing behind the pockmarked moon. The video was shot on an iPhone 17 Pro Max that the astronauts carried on the mission.

Article Title: Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth: When I start to experience the stress, that's a ...
Article Text:
Meta CTO shared coping strategies for stress

Meta CTO to engineers looking to join technology industry

Poll What is your primary method for coping with stress? Exercise Meditation Talking to friends/family





Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, who oversees the company’s Reality Labs division, says that stress is not something he encounters often, but when it surface, he treats it as a signal to reprioritise. According to a report by Business Insider, speaking during an Instagram ‘ask-me-anything’ session, Bosworth explained that he usually feels stressed only four or five times a year, and the trigger is almost always the same: being over-scheduled. Bosworth added that when he starts to feel stress, it’s mainly because he worries he won’t have enough tome for the ‘important work’. He described busyness as the root cause, noting: “When I start to experience the stress, that’s a useful signal for me. What is the important work that, if you dedicated the time to it, you would be okay? And, how do we reprioritise the urgent stuff that’s being noisy?”Bosworth also talked about some coping mechanisms which help to get out of a stressful situation. He explained that in order to manage stress he relies on conventional methods such as deep breathing, exercise, and spending time with his wife and kids. He also talks about stress opening, often with his wide, which he says helps him to reset. While he doesn’t consider his strategies unique, he emphasised that they are effective in keeping him grounded.Executives across industries use different approaches to cope with high-pressure roles.Some CEOs practice yoga, take long walks, or spend time in nature. Meta’s CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously told podcaster Joe Rogan that waking up and checking his phone felt like being “punched in the stomach,” and he manages stress by exercising in the mornings.Recently, Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth had a simple answer when a student asked how to enter the tech industry: stop waiting and start building. During an Instagram “Ask Me Anything” session this week, Bosworth was asked by a college freshman what it takes to break into technology. His response was direct and practical — focus less on theory and more on doing. “You just have to immerse yourself in it,” Bosworth said. “You should just constantly be building.”“That's what's going to give you the best chance of having the relevant skill set that is needed to make a difference in technology,” he said.
Article Summary: Andrew Bosworth, Meta’s chief technology officer, says stress is not something he encounters often, but when it surface, he treats it as a signal to reprioritise. The trigger is almost always the same: being over-scheduled. Bosworth also talks about stress opening, often with his wide, which he says helps to reset.

Article Title: Seiko US website breach: Hackers post ‘urgent security’ warning
Article Text:
Hackers claim access to Seiko customer data

Ransom demand and deadline

No confirmation from Seiko yet

Poll What do you think is the biggest threat to online shopping security? Data breaches Phishing scams Weak passwords Lack of encryption





The US website of premium smartwatch maker Seiko was hacked over the weekend, with attackers replacing part of the site with a message claiming a data breach. According to Bleeping Computer report, visitors to the “Press Lounge” section were shown a page titled “HACKED,” instead of the normal content. The message alleged that hackers had accessed the company’s Shopify system and stolen customer data. The message was reportedly later removed from the website.The defaced page included a warning that read: “This is an urgent security notification regarding your Shopify store. Your customer database has been compromised.”The attackers further claimed: “We have successfully breached your Shopify store's security systems and downloaded the entire customer database.”According to the message, the alleged stolen data may include names, email addresses, phone numbers, order history, shipping details and account-related information.The message also included a warning that the data would be made public if the company did not respond. The attackers said Seiko USA had 72 hours to begin negotiations.They also instructed the company to locate a specific customer account in its Shopify system, where a contact email had reportedly been added for communication.The identity of the attackers remains unknown. Seiko USA has not publicly responded to the incident or confirmed any data breach so far. The message has since been taken down, and the affected section of the website appears to have been restored.
Article Summary: The US website of premium smartwatch maker Seiko was hacked over the weekend. Hackers replaced part of the site with a message claiming a data breach. The message alleged that hackers had accessed the company’s Shopify system and stolen customer data.

Article Title: iOS 27: Apple’s iOS 27 may add redesigned Siri app with extensions support
Article Text:
Apple's Siri

Siri Extensions: An AI App Store



Hardware still remains at the core of Apple







Apple is preparing to unveil its next major software update — iOS 27 at its upcoming Worldwide Developers Conference scheduled to take place on June 8. According to a report by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman the company is expected to introduced a redesigned Siri app with a new “Extentions” feature with iOS 27 update. The report suggests that this move of the Cupertino-based tech giant is reflects its acknowledgement that its in-house AI technology lags behind rivals like ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Anthropic. Rather than competing head-to-head in the AI arms race, Apple is focusing on leveraging its hardware ecosystem and App Store model.As per the Bloomberg report, the new Extensions feature will enable the users to install third-party AI chatbots apart from ChatGPT and run them directly inside Siri. Along with this, Apple is said to create a dedicated App Store section for these integrations, effectively launching an AI marketplace. This approach of the company mirrors its historical strategy: offering built-in apps while allowing third-party alternatives, and taking a cut of revenue through its 30% App Store commission.Apple is also working closely with Google’s Gemini technology in order to revamp Siri and other AI functions, making sure that its own tools remain usable out of the box.But the company recognizes that most users won’t pay for Siri alone, so third-party integrations are seen as the key to keeping iPhone users engaged while monetizing AI-driven apps.Despite its AI push, Apple’s strength remains in hardware. Its chips and devices are well-suited to running AI workloads, and future product categories — from high-end AirPods and smart glasses to smart home devices — will rely on Siri’s evolution. Analysts note this reinforces Apple’s hardware-first identity and positions John Ternus, head of hardware engineering, as a potential successor to CEO Tim Cook.Unlike competitors aggressively monetizing AI subscriptions, Apple has effectively conceded the race to build the most advanced models. Instead, it is betting that Siri Extensions will keep users loyal to iPhones while letting third parties innovate. The strategy echoes the success of the App Store but raises questions about whether Apple can afford to let rivals define the future of AI while it focuses on ecosystem control.
Article Summary: Apple is preparing to unveil its next major software update — iOS 27. The company is expected to introduced a redesigned Siri app with a new “Extentions” feature. The new Extensions feature will enable the users to install third-party AI chatbots apart from ChatGPT and run them directly inside Siri. Along with this, Apple is said to create a dedicated App Store section for these integrations, effectively launching an AI marketplace.

Article Title: Amid layoffs, Mark Zuckerberg's Meta is asking engineers to use AI for…
Article Text:
Meta has set minimum AI coding targets for engineers across divisions, with some teams expected to write over 75% of their code using AI tools like Metamate and Google Gemini. The company has also restructured parts of Reality Labs into small AI "pods" with new job titles. The AI push comes alongside layoffs that hit hundreds of employees this week across multiple divisions.

Meta wants 80% of mid-to-senior engineers using AI tools like Gemini and Metamate

Meta Reality Labs restructured into small AI 'pods' with new job titles

The push comes as Meta cuts hundreds of jobs

Meta isn't just talking about going "AI-native"—it's putting numbers on it. Internal documents reviewed by Business Insider reveal that the company has set specific targets for how much of its engineers' code should be written with AI assistance, with some teams expected to hit as high as 75%. The push comes as Meta lays off hundreds of employees and doubles down on artificial intelligence across its operations.The targets vary by division. Meta's creation org—the team behind core experiences like Messenger, WhatsApp, and Facebook—has set a first-half 2026 goal requiring 65% of its engineers to write more than 75% of their committed code using AI coding tools. The Scalable Machine Learning team, which handles AI models and infrastructure, had a February 2026 target of 50% to 80% AI-assisted code.A companywide Q4 2025 goal for central products called for 55% of software engineers' code changes to be "agent-assisted. "It's not just about code volume. The company also set a target for 80% of mid-to-senior-level engineers to adopt AI tools like DevMate, Metamate, and Google's Gemini. The focus here, according to the document, is on tool adoption rather than raw output percentages. A Meta spokesperson told Business Insider that the company's performance programme rewards impact from AI tools, not just usage.Whether these AI adoption targets are formally tied to performance reviews remains unclear. But the direction is unmistakable. Last November, Meta's head of people Janelle Gale told employees in an internal memo that "AI-driven impact" would become a core expectation starting 2026. Workers were told to highlight their AI wins in self-reviews, and Meta rolled out an "AI Performance Assistant" to help employees write their own reviews.The coding targets are one piece of a larger organisational overhaul at Meta. A separate leaked memo showed that a 1,000-person division within Reality Labs has been reorganised into small "pods," with every employee now holding one of three titles—AI Builder, AI Pod Lead, or AI Org Lead. The structure is designed to flatten hierarchies and push cross-disciplinary work, with engineers potentially taking on design tasks depending on the project.Meta's CTO Andrew Bosworth has also taken direct charge of the company's "AI for Work" initiative, which drives internal AI tool adoption company-wide.All of this is happening against a backdrop of job cuts. Meta laid off hundreds of employees this week across Reality Labs, recruitment, and other divisions. The company said the restructuring and the layoffs are unrelated—but the optics are hard to ignore. Meta is simultaneously shrinking headcount and demanding that the engineers who remain lean harder on AI to pick up the slack. CEO Mark Zuckerberg himself framed 2026 as the year AI begins to replace what once required large teams with one "very talented" person.
Article Summary: Meta has set minimum AI coding targets for engineers across divisions. Some teams expected to write over 75% of their code using AI tools like Metamate and Google Gemini. The company has also restructured parts of Reality Labs into small AI "pods" with new job titles.

DeepSeek outage lasted several hours, users report disruptions

DeepSeek outage comes amid expectations of new update

China’s DeepSeek faced its biggest outage since its debut, with the AI chatbot doing down for several hours overnight. The disruption affected users across China, with many reporting issues accessing the service. According to user reports tracked on website health tracking platform Downdetector, problems began on Sunday (March 29) evening. DeepSeek later confirmed the issue on its status page, saying it detected a fault at around 9:35 p.m. The company initially marked the issue as resolved within two hours, but further updates showed continued performance problems before a fix was rolled out the next morning.DeepSeek said it implemented a fix around 9:13 a.m. on Monday and is monitoring the situation.The company did not share details about the cause of the outage. According to its official status page, all systems are now operational.The extended downtime is unusual for DeepSeek, which has maintained a high uptime record since the launch of its R1 model in January 2025. The platform has been seen as a key player in China’s AI space.The outage comes as speculation grows about a possible major update from DeepSeek. The company has not confirmed any timeline, but rivals such as Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have recently launched new AI models. Expectations remain high for DeepSeek’s next move, even as it stays silent on future plans.
Article Summary: China’s DeepSeek faced its biggest outage since its debut, with the AI chatbot doing down for several hours overnight. The disruption affected users across China, with many reporting issues accessing the service. The company said it implemented a fix around 9:13 a.m. on Monday and is monitoring the situation. According to its official status page, all systems are now operational.

Article Title: Sergey Brin: Google cofounder Sergey Brin to employees at town hall: This year a big role will be played by ...
Article Text:
Google cofounder Sergey Brin’s message to employees



AI adoption becoming mandatory at Google







Google employees have been buzzing about a new internal tool called Agent Smith, named after the antagonist in The Matrix. The tool is created to automate various tasks such as coding, run asynchronously in the background, and even be accessed via mobile phones. According to a report by Business Insider, the tool became so popular within the organisation that Google had to restrict access in order to manage demand. Designed on Google’s existing agentic coding platform Antigravity, Agent Smith interacts with various internal tools and can autonomously plan and execute workflows. Unlike earlier assistants, it can pull up documents linked to employee profiles and be used directly from Google’s internal chat platform.As per the BI reports, at a recent town hall with sales employees, Google cofounder Sergey Brin also stressed on the fact that AI agents will be a major focus for Google in 2026. Brin, who returned to hands-on work at Google in 2023, said agents represent the next big leap in productivity. Along with this, he also hinted at tools similar to “OpenClaw,” though it’s unclear if he was referring to Agent Smith or another project.In the same meeting, Google’s business chief Philipp Schindler also joked that he could tell when Brin’s agent was responding to messages on his behalf underscoring how deeply these tools are already being integrated into workflows.Google’s leadership, including CEO Sundar Pichai, has been pressing employees to adopt AI tools across technical and non-technical roles. In some cases, usage of AI is now factored into performance reviews. Engineers were told last year that AI adoption was expected, and more recently, non-technical staff have been given the same directive.Beyond top-down mandates, employees themselves are experimenting with initiatives like Project EAT, aimed at standardizing AI adoption across Google’s infrastructure teams.The comments made by Brin highlight the boarder industry trend as Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is also reportedly building his own AI agent to help run the company, while other tech giants are racing to embed agentic AI into daily operations.
Article Summary: Google employees have been buzzing about a new internal tool called Agent Smith, named after the antagonist in The Matrix. The tool is created to automate various tasks such as coding, run asynchronously in the background, and even be accessed via mobile phones. According to a report by Business Insider, the tool became so popular within the organisation that Google had to restrict access in order to manage demand.

Article Title: Who is John Ternus, Tim Cook’s successor, and Apple’s new CEO
Article Text:
John Ternus Apple CEO

John Ternus’ early career and rise within Apple

What challenges await Apple's next CEO

Poll Which product innovation do you think had the biggest impact? iPad AirPods iPhone MacBook Neo





Apple has announced that John Ternus will succeed Tim Cook to become the new CEO on September 1, 2026. Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years, will now transition to the role of executive chairman for the board. Ternus, who is Apple’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, has been a central figure in shaping the company’s most iconic products and is now been tasked with steering Apple into its next era of innovation. The transition, which was approved unanimously by the Board of Directors, follows a thoughtful, long-term succession planning process. Cook will continue in his role as CEO through the summer as he works closely with Ternus on a smooth transition. As executive chairman, Cook will assist with certain aspects of the company, including engaging with policymakers around the world.John Ternus joined Apple in 2001 as part of the product design team and became a vice president of Hardware Engineering in 2013. He brought with him a strong background in mechanical engineering. He earned his Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and started his career at Virtual Research Systems, where he worked on advanced virtual reality technologies.Throughout his tenure at Apple, Ternus has overseen hardware engineering work on a variety of groundbreaking products across every category. He was instrumental in the introduction of multiple new product lines, including iPad and AirPods, as well as many generations of products across iPhone, Mac, and Apple Watch. Ternus’s work on Mac has helped the category become more powerful and more popular globally than at any time in its 40-year history. That includes the recent introduction of MacBook Neo, an all-new laptop that makes the Mac experience even more accessible to more people around the world.This past fall, his team’s efforts were on full display with the introduction of a redefined iPhone lineup, including the incredibly powerful iPhone 17 Pro and Pro Max, the radically thin and durable iPhone Air, and the iPhone 17, which has been an incredible upgrade for users.With John Ternus as the new CEO, Apple gets a hardware leader at a pivotal moment. While Apple has seen tremendous success with iPhones and products like AirPods, the company has struggled in areas such as mixed reality, generative AI, the smart home and autonomous vehicles, according to Bloomberg.Apple has been spending significantly less on AI investments compared to rivals Meta, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. The company's AI-enabled Siri, originally slated for 2025, has been delayed until 2026 or later due to technical challenges. Apple has also lost several senior AI team members since January, many of whom have joined Meta's AI and Superintelligence Labs during talent wars this year.Despite these concerns, Apple is expecting strong end-of-year sales, with year-on-year revenue growth of 10% to 12% for its holiday quarter ending in December, fueled by the iPhone 17 model release. The company's shares are trading close to an all-time high following strong October earnings, though its roughly 12% stock price increase this year lags behind Big Tech rivals Alphabet, Nvidia and Microsoft.The reshuffle extends past the corner office. Johny Srouji, Apple's silicon architect, has been promoted to chief hardware officer, absorbing Ternus's old portfolio. Tom Marieb takes over day-to-day hardware engineering and reports to Srouji. Ternus joins Apple's board on September 1. Arthur Levinson, the company's non-executive chairman for the past 15 years, becomes lead independent director on the same day.
Article Summary: John Ternus will succeed Tim Cook to become the new CEO on September 1, 2026. Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years, will now transition to the role of executive chairman for the board. The transition follows a thoughtful, long-term succession planning process.

Article Title: Amazon files lawsuit after losing millions in fake laptop and GPU refunds; tells court: We spent over $75,000 investigating so give us ...
Article Text:
How the refund scam worked

How Amazon found out the fraud

Amazon spent $75,000-plus in investigation

Poll Do you trust online refund processes after hearing about scams like the RBK case? Yes No





A group of scammers stole $4 million worth of products from Amazon, including graphics cards and laptops, by duping customer support through an elaborate refund scam. Amazon has filed a lawsuit in Washington state District Court accusing a group using the name RBK of getting millions in refunds for products that were never returned. A group called RBK has been selling services to help Amazon customers commit reimbursement fraud by exploiting how the company issues refunds for packages that aren't delivered, according to the 41-page complaint. The group has been targeting Amazon in the US, Canada, and Europe, as well as other retailers.The complaint names Dias Temirbekul Zhumaniyaz, Michael Bauschelt and Adnan Islam as defendants, along with up to 20 unknown people from Amazon who are said to be involved in the operation.In its lawsuit, Amazon says that RBK promoted its refund service through the Telegram app and built a following of more than 1,000 subscribers. The company says RBK posted more than 2,100 user testimonials, or “vouches,” showing fraudulent Amazon refunds. Since February 2023, RBK has posted over $4,000,000.00 in vouchers for Amazon refunds,” the lawsuit adds. Amazon's lawsuit says that RBK also used Amazon’s name and logos in Telegram posts in a way that could make the service appear legitimate at first glance.According to the complaint, RBK users would buy items from Amazon, then contact the group through the Telegram app and share their Amazon login information. As per Amazon's complaint, RBK charged users 15% to 30% of the order cost, contacted customer service while posing as the buyer and claimed that the shipment arrived as an empty package.In certain cases, Amazon said that to dupe customer support into believing the claim, RBK will use elaborate, fake police reports about the missing package, among other social engineering techniques.Amazon uncovered the fraud refund scam after a company investigator posed as a buyer interested in RBK’s services. An investigator bought a PlayStation Portal Remote Player for $199 plus $13.18 in tax, then paid RBK $100 in bitcoin after the item was delivered. Amazon says the investigator later received a refund after RBK told customer service that the product had not been received and provided a police report.The company’s investigation also found a video and an image on the RBK Telegram channel that appeared to reveal the mailing address and phone number of the alleged administrator of the scam group, Kazakhstan-based Dias Temirbekul Zhumaniyaz.“Amazon’s internal investigators used this information to identify login information, including IP addresses located in Kazakhstan, that were used to access Amazon accounts associated with Zhumaniyaz,” the company says. The same login information was connected to other customer accounts involved in the refund fraud, including for high-value items such as laptops and drones, as well as two PowerColor AMD Radeon RX 7900 XT Graphics Cards for $2,054.43. ”The company says it spent more than $75,000 investigating the alleged scheme and is asking the court for damages and injunctive relief. Amazon has asked the court that the suspects should be told to pay restitution for the fraud and cover the company’s attorney fees in the lawsuit. The e-commerce provider is also urging the court to block the defendants from using Telegram or Amazon trademarks to engage in similar schemes.
Article Summary: Group called RBK has been selling services to help Amazon customers commit refund fraud. The group has been targeting Amazon in the US, Canada, and Europe, as well as other retailers. Amazon says it spent more than $75,000 investigating the alleged scheme and is asking the court for damages.

Article Title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over in September
Article Text:
A defining era under Cook

Ternus steps in with product focus

Poll How do you feel about Tim Cook stepping down as CEO of Apple? Excited for new leadership Concerned about future direction





Apple has announced a major leadership transition, with Tim Cook set to step down as chief executive in September after more than a decade at the helm. The 65-year-old will hand over to long-time company insider John Ternus, who will take charge of the $4 trillion tech giant on September 1.Cook, who succeeded Steve Jobs in 2011, will remain closely involved as executive chairman. In a statement, he described leading Apple as “the greatest privilege of my life”, marking the end of a tenure that saw the company’s valuation surge by more than $3.6 trillion.Cook’s leadership reshaped Apple into one of the world’s most valuable and operationally efficient companies. Having joined in 1998, he rose through the ranks by mastering the firm’s global supply chain and later took over as CEO during a period of uncertainty following Jobs’ resignation.Under his watch, Apple expanded its ecosystem beyond the iPhone, strengthening services and launching products such as AirPods and the Apple Watch. The company also became the first publicly traded firm to cross the $1 trillion valuation mark, eventually reaching $4 trillion.However, critics often pointed out that while Cook excelled at scaling and refining existing products, Apple did not produce a breakthrough innovation comparable to the iPhone during his tenure.Ternus, 50, brings a markedly different background. As Apple’s senior vice-president of hardware engineering, he has overseen the development of flagship products including the iPhone, Mac and newer devices such as the Vision Pro headset. Having spent 25 years at the company, he has been deeply involved in nearly every major product line.His appointment signals a potential shift in strategy, with Apple expected to lean more heavily into hardware innovation.The leadership change comes at a pivotal moment. Apple has faced criticism for lagging behind rivals in AI, even as competitors like Google and OpenAI push ahead rapidly. The company has already turned to external partnerships to bolster capabilities, including integrating advanced AI tools into its ecosystem.
Article Summary: Tim Cook, 65, will step down as CEO of Apple on September 1. Long-time company insider John Ternus will take charge of the $4 trillion tech giant. Cook succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011, but will remain executive chairman.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Sam Altman: “I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology... Very soon the world is going to have to contend with incredible video models that can deepfake an
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Sam Altman

Quote of the day by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman



“I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology... Very soon the world is going to have to contend with incredible video models that can deepfake anyone or kind of show anything you want.”



What the quote by Sam Altman highlights



Understanding deepfake technology



Why advanced video models are a concern



Impact on information and trust



Real-world risks associated with deepfakes



Efforts to manage and regulate AI risks



The role of public awareness



How AI continues to evolve



Other famous quotes by Sam Altman



“AI will probably most likely lead to the end of the world, but in the meantime, there’ll be great companies.”

“We are entering the age of intelligence.”

“The development of superhuman machine intelligence is probably the greatest threat to the continued existence of humanity.”

“Technology doesn’t solve problems by itself; people decide how it is used.”

A broader perspective on technology and responsibility



Final takeaway from Sam Altman's quote







People all over the world are talking more and more about the pros and cons of artificial intelligence as it continues to grow quickly. AI is a part of life now. It has things like writing, designing, and data analysis tools, as well as systems that can make videos and pictures. Experts are also worried about how these new technologies might be misused. Sam Altman , the CEO of OpenAI, is one person who has openly talked about the risks of very advanced AI systems. A lot of people are interested in what he said about deepfake technology and video models because they bring up a real and growing problem. It might be harder to tell what's real and what's fake as AI-generated content becomes more lifelike. This is a big deal for everyone, including people, businesses, and governments.This statement brings up a specific worry: the wrong use of AI to make videos. It says that the technology is new and powerful, but it can also be dangerous if you don't take the right safety precautions. Things that could hurt people, like lying, stealing someone's identity, or changing how people feel about something, are what "really bad stuff" means.The quote talks about how advanced AI systems can make videos that look real but might not show what really happened.This makes it harder for people to check facts and increases the chance of misunderstanding or abuse.Deepfake technology uses AI to change or make videos and audio look real. These systems learn how someone looks and talks by looking at a lot of pictures, videos, and voices. Once they have been trained, they can make new content that looks and acts a lot like the person they are copying.A deepfake video can make it look like someone did or said something they didn't really do. In the past, it was easy to find videos like these. But as AI models have gotten better, the quality has become much more realistic.The quote is about a worry that comes from how fast things are changing. It's getting harder for the average person to find fake content as technology gets better.AI can now make whole scenes instead of just edited clips, thanks to advanced video models. These systems can walk and talk like real people, take pictures that look real, and show feelings that look real.You can use these kinds of skills in a lot of different ways, like making movies, teaching, and simulating. You can also use the same tools to make content that is false or misleading.A fake but realistic video of a famous person could go viral on social media and change people's minds before it is proven to be false. People who know a lot about this say it's one of the most dangerous things. The quote's use of the phrase "show anything you want" shows how this could be used for bad things.Deepfake technology makes people less trusting, which is one of the worst things about it. A lot of people these days learn things by watching videos and looking at pictures. It's hard to know what's real when these sources are easy to change.This could change the way people talk to each other, how news is reported, and how evidence is used in court. If people start to doubt the truth of all digital content, they might not trust reliable sources as much.People are worried about more than just false information spreading; they're also worried about how it will change how society checks the truth in general.There are a lot of real-world risks that come with advanced deepfake technology. Identity theft is when someone uses someone's picture or voice without their permission. Misinformation is when false information is shared as true.Some people have used deepfake videos to spread false political messages or to pretend to be someone else in a money scam. These risks could get worse as the technology gets better if the right safety measures aren't taken.The quote shows that people know about these things happening in real life and need to be ready for them.Governments, tech companies, and research groups are all working to fix the problems that deepfakes and advanced AI systems are causing. Some of the work being done includes making rules about how to use these kinds of technologies and making tools that can find fake content.Some platforms are also putting labels or warnings on content made by AI to make it easier for people to understand what they are looking at. Researchers are also looking into ways to watermark content to show that it was made by AI.The goal of these steps is to stop people from using the technology for bad things while still letting people use it for good things.Another important part of keeping AI safe is making sure that the public knows about it. As technology changes, people need to know how it works and how it can be used for bad things.People can be more careful when they look at things online if they know about deepfake technology. One way to stop false information from spreading is to check multiple sources, make sure the information is correct, and be careful about sharing videos that haven't been checked.The quote indirectly shows how important it is to be aware by pointing out how real and powerful these technologies are becoming.Experts say that AI will keep getting better over the next few years. It looks like video generation models will get faster, more detailed, and easier to use.This progress will bring both good and bad things. It can make it easier to talk to people, learn, and be creative, on the other hand. It might also make the need for stronger protections and responsible use even more important.Sam Altman's statement shows how to find a middle ground between being careful and trying new things.People are talking about deepfakes as part of a bigger conversation about how society handles powerful technologies. New things that people make have always had both good and bad effects. The most important thing has always been how they are used and controlled.The rapid growth of AI makes this process more difficult. People who work for the government, businesses, researchers, and users all need to work together.The quote reminds us that new ideas can help us move forward, but we need to plan and use them carefully to avoid problems.Sam Altman's statement brings up a very important point about the digital world we live in today: the growing power of AI-generated content and the risks that come with it. The quote talks about how things can be misused, which makes people more aware of their own safety, the rules, and safety in general.As AI gets better, it's important to know both the good and bad things about it. It is now possible to make realistic digital content, which opens up new possibilities. However, systems are also needed to make sure that the content is correct, reliable, and used properly.
Article Summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman: "I expect some really bad stuff to happen because of the technology" The quote talks about how advanced AI systems can make videos that look real but might not show what really happened. It might be harder to tell what's real and what's fake as AI-generated content becomes more lifelike.
